title: Calgary Restricted Development Area Regulations
text:
(Consolidated up to 87/2025) 


ALBERTA REGULATION 212/76 


Government Organization Act 


CALGARY RESTRICTED DEVELOPMENT 

AREA REGULATIONS 











1    These
Regulations may be cited as the “Calgary Restricted Development Area
Regulations”. 


AR 212/76 s1 


2    In these Regulations 


                                 (a)    “Area”
means the Calgary Restricted Development Area established under these
Regulations; 


                                 (b)    “government
agency” means 


                                           (i)    a
corporation that is an agent of the Crown in right of Alberta, or 


                                          (ii)    any
corporation, commission, board or other body empowered to exercise quasi‑judicial
or governmental functions and whose members are appointed by an Act of the
Legislature, the Lieutenant Governor in Council, or a Minister of the Crown, or
any combination thereof; 


                                 (c)    “Minister”
means the Minister of Infrastructure; 


                                 (d)    “surface
disturbance” means 


                                           (i)    the
disturbance, exposure, covering or erosion of the surface of land in any
manner, or 


                                          (ii)    the
degradation or deterioration in any manner of the physical surface of the land.



AR 212/76
s2;206/2001;105/2005;68/2008 


3    The lands described in the Schedules
attached to these Regulations are hereby established as a Restricted
Development Area called the “Calgary Restricted Development Area”. 


AR 212/76 s3;371/85 


4 (1)   No
Minister of the Crown or government official shall, without the written consent
of the Minister of Infrastructure, grant or cause to be granted any estate or
interest in lands of the Crown in the area pursuant to the Mines and
Minerals Act , the Public Lands Act or any other Act. 


(2)   No Minister of the Crown, government official
or government agency shall, without the written consent of the Minister of
Infrastructure, exercise any power under the Municipal Government Act , The
Pipe Line Act, 1975 , the Water Resources Act or any other Act to
order, authorize, approve, permit or consent to any operation or activity that
causes, is likely to cause or will cause a surface disturbance of any land in
the Area, or issue or cause to be issued any order, authorization, approval,
permit, licence or consent instrument for that purpose. 


(3)   No Minister of the Crown, government official
or government agency shall, without the written consent of the Minister of
Infrastructure, exercise any power under the Planning Act, 


                                 (a)    approving
a subdivision (as defined in that Act), or 


                                 (b)    approving,
consenting or issuing any instrument, 


affecting
any land located within the Area. 


AR 212/76
s4;206/2001;49/2004;105/2005;68/2008 


5 (1)   In this
section “geophysical operations” means any work or acts done in connection with
or incidental to the investigation of the subsurface by 


                                 (a)    seismic
operations, or 


                                 (b)    gravimetric
operations, or 


                                 (c)    magnetic
operations, or 


                                 (d)    electrical
operations, or 


                                 (e)    geochemical
operations, or 


                                 (f)    test
drilling, or 


                                 (g)    any
other operations or method employed to determine geologic or other conditions
underlying the surface of land, 


but does not include a geological
survey unless holes are drilled or are to be drilled to a depth in excess of 20
feet. 


(2)   No person, shall without the written consent of
the Minister, continue, commence or recommence any operation or activity that
causes, or will likely cause any surface disturbance of land in the Area or to
construct or erect any buildings on any land in the Area, and without limiting
the generality of the foregoing, no person shall, without the consent of the
Minister, continue, commence or recommence any operation or activity that is of
the kind falling within the following descriptions: 


                                 (a)    the
opening up, operation or abandonment of a mine or quarry; 


                                 (b)    the
construction, operation or abandonment of a pipe line, battery, transmission
line or telecommunication line; 


                                 (c)    the
drilling, operation or abandonment of a well; 


                                 (d)    the
opening up, operation or abandonment of a pit or of a waste disposal site or
land fill site; 


                                 (e)    geophysical
operations that result or will result in surface disturbance; 


                                 (f)    the
construction, operation or abandonment of a mineral processing plant, road,
railway or aircraft landing strip; 


                                 (g)    the
construction, operation or abandonment of any structure (including a micro‑wave
tower) forming part of a broadcasting undertaking as defined in the Broadcasting
Act (Canada); 


                                 (h)    the
construction, operation or abandonment of a site for subsurface disposal of
solid or liquid waste; 


                                  (i)    the
conduct of examination and surveys on land made in connection with water or
conduct of exploration in respect of surface and subsurface water; 


                                  (j)    the
construction of any works for the impoundment, conveying or diversion of water; 


                                 (k)    the
preparation of land to be used for the purpose of residential, commercial or
industrial sites or for recreational development; 


                                  (l)    the
excavation and removal of top soil or peat for the purpose of sale; 


                               (m)    the
excavation and removal of archeological findings; 


                                 (n)    any
alteration of or extension of any operation or activity referred to in clauses
(a) to (m). 


AR 212/76 s5 


6    Notwithstanding section 5, subsection (2)
where, upon these Regulations coming into force, there is within the Area land
upon which there is located 


                                 (a)    a
retail business, or 


                                 (b)    a
wholesale business, or 


                                 (c)    an
office, or 


                                 (d)    a
warehouse, or 


                                 (e)    an
agricultural operation, or 


                                 (f)    an
operation which is engaged in the 


                                           (i)    manufacturing
of goods, or 


                                          (ii)    processing
of materials, or 


                                 (g)    a
facility at which equipment, machinery or materials in bulk are stored or
stockpiled, or 


                                 (h)    a
facility or area used for recreation, or 


                                  (i)    buildings
or other structures used for or in connection with a residence, 


that land use may be continued
without the written consent of the Minister. 


AR 212/76 s6 


7    The Minister’s consent may be
unconditional or may be subject to such conditions as in the Minister’s opinion
will minimize or alleviate any adverse effect on land in the Area and may
require the person proposing to conduct or conducting any operation or activity
on the land to file with the Minister for approval a development and
reclamation plan containing such reports, plans, maps, sketches or other
information as the Minister may prescribe. 


AR 212/76 s7 


8 (1)   The
Minister 


                                 (a)    may
acquire by purchase or expropriation any estate or interest in any land within
the Area, or 


                                 (b)    may
order the removal of any buildings, improvements, materials or animals from the
Area and authorize the payment of compensation by the Crown to any person for
any loss resulting therefrom to that person. 


(2)   Subject to subsection (3), the administration
of land acquired under this section is hereby transferred to the Minister of
Environment and Protected Areas. 


(3)   The administration of those portions of land
acquired under this section that consist of 


                                 (a)    any
area of the land on which a building is situated and any area of the land that
is appurtenant to and necessary or convenient for the use and enjoyment of that
building, and 


                                 (b)    any
area of land over which the Minister, for any other reason, deems it advisable
that he have the power of administration 


is
hereby reserved to the Minister. 


AR 212/76
s8;53/82;206/2001;170/2012;216/2022 


9    All of the provisions of the Surface
Rights Act are inapplicable to lands of the Crown within the Area. 


AR 212/76 s9 


10    All provisions of the Expropriation
Act are inapplicable to lands of the Crown within the area. 


AR 212/76 s10 


11    Any person who is guilty of an offence
under these Regulations is liable on summary conviction 











                                 (a)    for
a first offence to a fine of not less than $1000 and not more than $10,000 and
in default of payment to imprisonment for a term of not more than 120 days, and 


                                 (b)    for
a second or subsequent offence, 


                                           (i)    in
the case of a natural person, to imprisonment for a term of not less than one
month and not more than six months, or 


                                          (ii)    in
the case of a corporation, to a fine of not less than $10,000 and not more than
$50,000. 


AR 212/76 s11

AR 218/89 s8 


Without special mention all intervening roads, road allowances and
public thoroughfares how so ever created which lie within the
Transportation/Utility Corridor Boundaries, as defined in Schedule A, are
deemed to be included within the Restricted Development Area.  Definition -
“L.S.D.” means “legal subdivision” as defined in the Alberta Surveys Act .
